author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
The Seventh Objection, of the Effect of Baptism.
title: The Seventh Objection, of the Effect of Baptism.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: Chapter 41 [XXIII.]—The Seventh Objection, of the Effect of Baptism.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: “We condemn,” says he, “those who affirm that baptism does not do away all sins, because we know that full cleansing is conferred by these mysteries.”

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: We also say this; but you do not say that infants are also by those same mysteries freed from the bonds of their first birth and of their hateful descent.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: On which account it behoves you, like other heretics also, to be separated from the Church of Christ, which holds this of old time.
